title: helictotrichon desertorum subsp. basalticum
intro: Helictotrichon desertorum subsp. basalticum, also known as Basalt Koeler's grass or Basalt red fescue, is a subspecies of grass within the Helictotrichon desertorum species. It is a type of ornamental grass commonly used in landscaping for its striking red or pink coloration during the winter months. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its expectorant properties, and is said to have medicinal properties that aid in relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The herbal preparations of Helictotrichon desertorum subsp. basalticum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote overall health and well-being, particularly in the respiratory system.
